page: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The system is configured with 768.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance includes a massive local storage allocation of 22,500 GB delivered via NVMe SSDs, making it highly suitable for data-intensive operations. Networking is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 56 Gbps, managed through the AWS Nitro hypervisor. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this instance.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance across multiple domains. The i7i.24xlarge achieves top-tier ratings in Passmark CPU tests, including extended instructions, encryption, and physics. Database operations and Redis SET operations both rank in the top tier, with Redis reaching 113.26 million operations per second. Memory bandwidth is strong, with uncached read speeds of 287.02 GB/sec and write speeds of 119.97 GB/sec. For LLM workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier prompt processing speeds for medium-sized 7B models and strong performance for larger 70B models, though text generation speeds for 70B models remain average.

Given its high resource density, combining 22.5 TB of local NVMe SSD storage with 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, the i7i.24xlarge offers strong qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive local storage throughput and high memory capacity without the overhead of external storage attachments. This server is highly optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput Redis caching layers, heavy data compilation, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized instance powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 768.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it features a massive 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The server delivers top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and multi-threaded decompression, alongside strong memory bandwidth. While it lacks a GPU, its advanced CPU instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, support efficient vector and matrix operations. This hardware profile makes the i7i.24xlarge highly cost-efficient for storage-heavy, memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale databases, high-throughput caching, and data-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.24xlarge is a storage-optimized server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized platform featuring massive local NVMe SSD capacity and advanced Intel Xeon processing power.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with AVX-512 and AMX instruction support
768 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
22,500 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier database, Redis, and multi-threaded decompression performance
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and data-intensive workloads
